pricing.question: How does pricing work?
pricing.answer: Start pay-per-event or choose an annual bundle for a busy season. Upgrade anytime from studio billing — launch promo codes apply at signup when billing goes live.

privacy.question: What about guest privacy and selfies?
privacy.answer: Guests consent before uploading a selfie. Selfies are used only for matching and are not stored in the gallery. See our Privacy Policy for full details.

setup.question: How long does setup take?
setup.answer: Most studios create their first client gallery in under ten minutes. Face indexing runs in the background after you upload the wedding album.

branding.question: Can I use my studio logo and colors?
branding.answer: Yes — each client gallery can reflect your studio branding so the guest experience feels like yours.

help.title: How Snapic works
help.subtitle: Quick answers for the bride & groom.

guestFlow.title: How will guests use this?
guestFlow.body: Send guests your link or QR. They take a quick selfie and instantly see every photo they appear in — no scrolling through hundreds of shots.

upload.title: How do I add photos?
upload.body: Tap 'Upload your wedding photos' on the next-step card. You can drag-and-drop or pick files from your phone. After upload, we index faces automatically.

partner.title: Can my partner help manage this?
partner.body: Yes — invite them in the 'Invite a co-admin' section. They'll receive an email and can upload photos and manage settings too.

indexing.title: Why aren't guests finding photos?
indexing.body: After every upload, Snapic needs a moment to scan faces. The next-step card will show you when indexing is finished and your gallery is ready to share.

preview.title: What will guests actually see?
preview.body: Tap 'Preview as guest' at the top of this page to open a live preview of your guest page — the same experience your friends and family will get.

support.title: Still stuck?
support.body: Reply to your approval email or reach out to your photographer — we're here to help on your big day.

setup.indexReadyNotice: {{result}} Your album is ready to go live.
setup.indexNoPhotos: No photos were indexed. Some images may be corrupted or missing faces — try re-uploading them.
setup.indexFailed: Face indexing failed. Wait a moment and try again, or upload clearer photos.

contact.title: Contact
contact.lead: We're here to help — especially on your wedding day.
contact.generalBody: For setup help, studio questions, or wedding-day issues:
contact.privacyBody: To access, correct, or delete your data, or ask about how we process information:
contact.weddingDayBody: If guests cannot search photos during a live event, email us with your event link — we prioritize live weddings.

sections.heading: Who we are
sections.body:
Snapic provides AI-powered wedding photo matching for guests, couples, and photographers in Israel.

This draft policy describes what we collect and why. Final version will include our registered business name and contact details.

sections.heading: What we collect
sections.body:
Guest selfies: processed in memory to find matches — not stored after the search completes.

Gallery photos: uploaded by couples or photographers; we store images and derived face embeddings to power matching.

Match metadata: search timestamps, match counts, optional share links (expire after 7 days), and anonymous session IDs for rate limiting and search history.

Account data: email and profile for signed-in users (couples, photographers, admins).

Signup requests: email, couple names, wedding date, optional message.

sections.heading: How we use data
sections.body:
To match guests with wedding photos, operate galleries, manage studio accounts, prevent abuse, and improve reliability.

We do not sell personal data. We do not use guest selfies for training third-party models.

sections.heading: Legal basis (Israel)
sections.body:
Processing is based on consent (guest selfie matching), contract performance (studio and couple services), and legitimate interests (security, fraud prevention).

Biometric-related processing (face embeddings) requires explicit guest consent before each search.

sections.heading: Third-party processors
sections.body:
Data may be processed by: Supabase (database, auth, storage), Render and Vercel (hosting), Sentry (error monitoring), Google Fonts (typography), and Stripe (billing, when enabled).

Some processors may store data outside Israel. We use providers with appropriate safeguards.

sections.heading: Retention
sections.body:
Share links expire after 7 days. Events auto-close guest access ~90 days after the wedding date; photos and embeddings remain until deleted by the event owner or Snapic.

Match run history for anonymous guests can be cleared from the guest page.

sections.heading: Your rights
sections.body:
You may request access, correction, or deletion of your personal data by emailing privacy@snapic.me.

Signed-in users can delete their account from studio settings. Guests can clear search history on the event page.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body:
Privacy inquiries: privacy@snapic.me

We will update this policy when our business registration is finalized and after legal review.

sections.heading: Agreement
sections.body:
By using Snapic you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.

Photographers and couples using paid features enter a separate commercial relationship governed by pricing and refund terms shown at purchase.

sections.heading: Service description
sections.body:
Snapic helps wedding guests find photos they appear in by uploading a selfie. Couples and photographers manage galleries, branding, and guest access.

Matching is automated and may not find every photo. Results depend on photo quality, lighting, and face visibility.

sections.heading: Account responsibilities
sections.body:
You must provide accurate information. You are responsible for activity under your account.

Photographers and couples must have the right to upload wedding photos and must inform guests that AI face matching is used at the event.

sections.heading: Acceptable use
sections.body:
Do not upload unlawful, abusive, or non-wedding content. Do not attempt to bypass rate limits, scrape the service, or misuse guest data.

We may suspend accounts that violate these terms.

sections.heading: Intellectual property
sections.body:
Photographers and couples retain ownership of uploaded photos. Snapic receives a limited license to host, process, and display photos solely to provide the service.

Snapic branding, software, and UI remain our property.

sections.heading: Limitation of liability
sections.body:
The service is provided as-is. To the extent permitted by Israeli law, Snapic is not liable for indirect damages, lost photos due to user error, or matching inaccuracies.

Our total liability is limited to fees paid in the 12 months before the claim.

sections.heading: Changes and contact
sections.body:
We may update these terms. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.

Questions: hello@snapic.me

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: Snapic uses browser local storage and similar technologies — not traditional third-party advertising cookies.

sections.heading: Essential storage
sections.body: Locale preference (snapic_locale), authentication session (via Supabase), anonymous guest session ID for search history and rate limiting, and cookie consent choice (snapic_cookie_consent).

sections.heading: Functional storage
sections.body: Studio organization selection, PWA install prompt dismissal, sidebar preferences, and marketing attribution (ref/UTM, session-only) when you arrive via a referral link.

sections.heading: Third parties
sections.body: Supabase auth may set cookies. Sentry may use storage for error diagnostics. Google Fonts are loaded from Google servers when you use the app.

sections.heading: Your choices
sections.body:
You can decline non-essential storage via the cookie banner. Clearing browser storage will reset preferences and sign you out.

See our Privacy Policy for data rights.

sections.heading: Commitment
sections.body:
Snapic aims to conform with Israeli Standard 5568 (WCAG 2.0 Level AA) for our public website and guest experience.

We support Hebrew RTL and English, keyboard navigation on key flows, and screen reader labels on primary actions.

sections.heading: Known limitations
sections.body:
Some modals and camera capture flows may have incomplete keyboard traps. We are actively improving focus management.

Third-party camera APIs depend on device and browser support.

sections.heading: Feedback
sections.body: If you encounter an accessibility barrier, contact hello@snapic.me with the page URL and description. We will respond within 5 business days.

sections.heading: Updates
sections.body: This statement will be updated as we complete a formal accessibility audit before general launch.

a11y.goToEvent: Go to one of your events
